name: docblock-rewrite
description: Converts PHPDoc and JSDoc blocks into one-line plain-English // comments that a non-technical reader can understand. Two modes: scripted bulk run via /docblock-rewrite slash command (bash + perl + claude CLI walks a directory, calls Claude per symbol, validates against banned-words list and 100-char cap, replaces in place with .bak backups) and skill-driven interactive mode for small files via Read/Edit. Skip list covers vendor/node_modules/dist/build/coverage/__tests__/.git/.svn/.hg/.next/.nuxt/out/tmp plus *.min.js and *.generated.* files. Honors @internal/@deprecated/@ignore opt-out tags. File-level detection via @package/@file/@module or post-block declare/namespace/use/import/export lines. Banned-word list: instantiate, invoke, callback, promise, iterate, async, boolean, array, object, parameter, argument, mutate, hash, payload, instance, factory, singleton, polyfill, regex. Output format: one line beginning with // and ending with a period, capitalized, present-tense verb for functions, noun phrase for classes/files/properties. Validation gates: one-line check, // prefix, length cap, banned-word regex. Bad outputs leave the original block intact for human review.
